poems.title: Lanterns in the Rain
poems.excerpt: We walked where the evening softened, and every drop was a tiny bell. The sky held its breath and the streets glowed like lanterns in the rain.
poems.author: Udaya Adhikari
poems.fullPoem:
We walked where the evening softened,
And every drop was a tiny bell.
The sky held its breath in silver,
And the streets glowed like lanterns in the rain.

Your silence was a warm umbrella,
My heart a pocket full of sparks.
We did not speak, yet all the music
Found its way between the dark.

poems.title: Paper Boats
poems.excerpt: I folded my wishes into small boats and set them down the gutter river. They floated past old laughter and new beginnings.
poems.author: Udaya Adhikari
poems.fullPoem:
I folded my wishes into small boats
And set them down the gutter river.
They floated past old laughter,
Past the gate where summers start.

If one returns with salt on its tongue,
I will know the sea remembers me.

poems.title: Windowlight
poems.excerpt: Morning spills its quiet across the room, and for a moment even the shadows are kind.
poems.author: Udaya Adhikari
poems.fullPoem:
Morning spills its quiet across the room,
A soft gold tide on the wooden floor.
For a moment even the shadows are kind,
And the world chooses gentleness.

I drink the light like a new promise,
And let the day begin.

poems.title: Letters Never Sent
poems.excerpt: There are letters I never sent, each one folded with care and held like a secret in the pocket of time.
poems.author: Udaya Adhikari
poems.fullPoem:
There are letters I never sent,
Each one folded with care.
Words I kept like pressed flowers
In the pocket of time.

If you ever find them,
Know they were written
With the patience of rain.

poems.title: After the Festival
poems.excerpt: The city quiets after the music, and the lantern strings sway like tired smiles.
poems.author: Udaya Adhikari
poems.fullPoem:
After the festival, the city quiets.
Lantern strings sway like tired smiles.
Footsteps fade into soft streets,
And the night folds itself in.

I walk home with your laughter
Still warm in my coat.

poems.title: The Hill of Quiet
poems.excerpt: We climbed until the noise fell away, and the wind taught us how to listen.
poems.author: Udaya Adhikari
poems.fullPoem:
We climbed until the noise fell away,
And the wind taught us how to listen.
Below, the valley gathered its lights.
Above, the sky opened like a book.

In that quiet, we learned
How vast a heartbeat can be.
